behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because the failure appears at module handoff (three collectors -> aggregator -> summary input), not inside one pure function. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit is too low (MOCK-MISS: this behavior is specifically about cross-module inclusion). System is rejected because behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess; source clients can be in-process fakes. Agentic is rejected because correctness is about payload composition before generation, not model output content. Workflow is rejected because no multi-agentic sequence is described.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real data-collection/orchestration modules with stubbed source adapters returning distinct markers per source; assert the exact payload sent to summary generation contains all three markers/fields. Inject one malformed-but-present source payload to ensure handoff logic still includes it rather than silently dropping.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, since resilience depends on interaction between fetch layer, section builder, and report assembler. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit is too low because the failure mode is boundary behavior (exception/empty result propagation across modules). System is rejected: behavior text only says a source is unavailable, which can be represented by an in-process timeout/exception fake; no real network stack is required. Agentic and Workflow are rejected because this behavior is fallback/orchestration, not model judgment or multi-agentic chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Run report assembly with two source adapters returning data and one adapter raising an unavailability error; assert process completes, failing section contains `data unavailable`, and remaining sections are present. Also assert no top-level abort/exception is emitted.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Agentic, because correctness depends on what the real summarizer outputs from provided values (LLM-DEP). PASS2-AUDIT: Integration is too low; deterministic stubs would always pass and cannot expose misrepresentation/hallucination. System is insufficient because real HTTP/process behavior is not the primary risk boundary; model output quality is. Workflow is rejected because behavior describes a single generation judgment, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixed input datasets with known key values and run the real summary-generation model; evaluate whether generated narrative preserves required facts/metrics (with tolerance rubric) across repeated runs to catch non-deterministic misstatements.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: System, because this behavior’s failure boundary includes real email delivery/inbox reception (REAL-INFRA). PASS2-AUDIT: Integration is too low; mocked email transport can hide auth/TLS/provider acceptance/inbox routing failures (MOCK-HIDE). Agentic considered but rejected by behavior text: success criterion is delivery to inbox, not summary semantic quality, so this is infrastructure-focused end-to-end validation. Workflow rejected: no required multi-agentic sequence is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script in an environment with real endpoints (or production-like test endpoints) and real email service credentials; assert message is accepted by provider and appears in configured inbox with expected subject/body structure and attachments/format.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because failure is state handoff across modules (delivery failure handler -> local persistence -> next-run retry logic). PASS2-AUDIT: Unit is too low since persistence and retry state span multiple components/runs. System considered but rejected: behavior text does not require real network outage mechanics or external scheduler; unreachable service can be simulated by adapter error, and local filesystem can be exercised in-process. Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior is transport fallback/retry, not model decision quality or multi-agentic chaining.
behaviors.test_description: First run: use a failing email adapter, generate report, assert fallback file is written with retry metadata. Second run: use working adapter, assert persisted report is retried/sent and fallback artifact is cleared or marked delivered.
